title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: tutorial goes end--end pipeline : extract tracts ancestry dosages munge external GWAS summary statistics run TLS-Tractor , walk step--step example continuous phenotype, provide runnable short script binary phenotype.

title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: examples tutorial use simulated files bundled package inst/extdata/. simulated data include genotype, covariates, phenotype unrelated two-way admixed individuals. Individuals split non-overlapping internal (n = 100) external (n = 8000) cohorts. external cohort, standard GWAS models (y ~ total genotype dosage + covariates) used generate external GWAS summary statistics. internal cohort provides individual-level covariates, phenotype data, local ancestry inference (LAI) results RFMix2/Gnomix FLARE formats.
code: # Get data directory path data_dir <- system.file("extdata", package = "tlstractor")  # To see all available simulated files, use: list.files(data_dir, full.names = FALSE) #>  [1] "simulate_binary.covar.txt"                  #>  [2] "simulate_binary.external_gwas_sumstats.txt" #>  [3] "simulate_binary.flare.vcf"                  #>  [4] "simulate_binary.internal_data.txt"          #>  [5] "simulate_binary.meta.txt"                   #>  [6] "simulate_binary.msp.tsv"                    #>  [7] "simulate_binary.phased.vcf"                 #>  [8] "simulate_binary.pheno.txt"                  #>  [9] "simulate_linear.covar.txt"                  #> [10] "simulate_linear.external_gwas_sumstats.txt" #> [11] "simulate_linear.flare.vcf"                  #> [12] "simulate_linear.internal_data.txt"          #> [13] "simulate_linear.meta.txt"                   #> [14] "simulate_linear.msp.tsv"                    #> [15] "simulate_linear.phased.vcf"                 #> [16] "simulate_linear.pheno.txt"

title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: linear example (simulate_linear.*) includes: simulate_linear.phased.vcf simulate_linear.msp.tsv Phased VCF stores genotypes GT field; MSP stores local ancestry labels haplotype window. simulate_linear.flare.vcf VCF GT:AN1:AN2 format GT denotes phased genotypes AN1 AN2 encode local ancestry labels haplotype. simulate_linear.pheno.txt simulate_linear.covar.txt simulate_linear.external_gwas_sumstats.txt Columns include CHR POS ID REF ALT BETA SE AF N.

title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: binary simulation file layout prefix simulate_binary: simulate_binary.phased.vcf simulate_binary.msp.tsv simulate_binary.flare.vcf simulate_binary.pheno.txt simulate_binary.covar.txt simulate_binary.external_gwas_sumstats.txt

what: Step 1: Extract tracts and convert formats
title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: step extracts local ancestry tracts LAI results converts formats flexible downstream analysis. downstream steps TLS-Tractor, GDS required format. First, create directory store tract outputs. Second, use one extractor based LAI format. RFMix2/Gnomix style phased VCF + MSP, use extract_tracts(). FLARE VCF, use extract_tracts_flare().
code: output_dir_tracts <- file.path(tempdir(),                                 "tlstractor_tutorial",                                 "linear", "tracts") dir.create(output_dir_tracts, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

previous_headings: Step 1: Extract tracts and convert formats > Generate GDS file (required for downstream steps)
what: Option A: RFMix2/Gnomix style (phased VCF + MSP)
title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: Use extract_tracts() local ancestry inference output phased VCF plus MSP file. vcf_path: phased VCF input phased genotypes (GT field required first FORMAT field) msp_path: MSP input local ancestry labels num_ancs: number ancestral populations dataset output_dir: directory output files (NULL uses input VCF directory) output_formats = "gds": write GDS output required downstream TLS-Tractor steps chunk_size: number variants processed per chunk (default: 1024L). Larger values can improve speed increase memory usage. GDS output path <prefix>.gds within output_dir, prefix basename input VCF file vcf_path without .vcf .vcf.gz.
code: extract_tracts(   vcf_path = file.path(data_dir, "simulate_linear.phased.vcf"),   msp_path = file.path(data_dir, "simulate_linear.msp.tsv"),   num_ancs = 2L,   output_dir = output_dir_tracts,   output_formats = "gds" )  # After running, the GDS file will be at: gds_path <- file.path(output_dir_tracts, "simulate_linear.phased.gds")

previous_headings: Step 1: Extract tracts and convert formats > Generate GDS file (required for downstream steps)
title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: Use extract_tracts_flare() local ancestry inference output FLARE VCF. vcf_path: FLARE VCF input GT:AN1:AN2 format num_ancs: number ancestral populations dataset output_dir: directory output files (NULL uses input VCF directory) output_formats = "gds": write GDS output required downstream TLS-Tractor steps chunk_size: number variants processed per chunk (default: 1024L). Larger values can improve speed increase memory usage. GDS output path <prefix>.gds within output_dir, prefix basename input VCF file vcf_path without .vcf .vcf.gz.
code: extract_tracts_flare(   vcf_path = file.path(data_dir, "simulate_linear.flare.vcf"),   num_ancs = 2L,   output_dir = output_dir_tracts,   output_formats = "gds" )  # After running, the GDS file will be at: gds_path <- file.path(output_dir_tracts, "simulate_linear.flare.gds")

previous_headings: Step 1: Extract tracts and convert formats > Generate GDS file (required for downstream steps)
title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: extraction, GDS file stores local ancestry risk allele information hierarchical structure optimized analysis. can think compact container two types content: sample IDs SNP metadata describe samples variants ancestry-specific arrays risk allele dosages haplotype counts structure, dosage contains number copies risk allele ancestry hapcount contains number haplotypes ancestry chunk demonstrates basic usage GDS files: reading sample IDs SNP metadata, extracting ancestry-specific dosage hapcount selected SNPs individuals. Benefits GDS format: Memory efficient: Uses compressed binary representation typically much smaller .vcf.gz .txt.gz, enabling -demand access without loading full dataset memory Fast access: Supports efficient sequential indexed random access across variants samples Portable: Stores genotypes, local ancestry, meta data single, self-contained file Scalable: Designed large-scale genomic datasets, enabling efficient analysis genome-wide data large cohorts
code: # Open the GDS file in read-only mode gds <- gdsfmt::openfn.gds(gds_path, readonly = TRUE)  # Display the GDS structure print(gds) #> File: /tmp/RtmpveE2wN/tlstractor_tutorial/linear/tracts/simulate_linear.flare.gds (3.5K) #> +    [  ] #> |--+ sample.id   { Str8 100 ZIP_ra(35.0%), 287B } #> |--+ snp.chromosome   { Str8 5 ZIP_ra(104.0%), 33B } #> |--+ snp.position   { Int32 5 ZIP_ra(170.0%), 41B } #> |--+ snp.id   { Str8 5 ZIP_ra(72.0%), 43B } #> |--+ snp.ref   { Str8 5 ZIP_ra(220.0%), 29B } #> |--+ snp.alt   { Str8 5 ZIP_ra(220.0%), 29B } #> |--+ dosage   [  ] #> |  |--+ anc0   { Bit2 100x5 LZ4_ra(119.2%), 156B } #> |  \--+ anc1   { Bit2 100x5 LZ4_ra(119.2%), 156B } #> \--+ hapcount   [  ] #>    |--+ anc0   { Bit2 100x5 LZ4_ra(119.2%), 156B } #>    \--+ anc1   { Bit2 100x5 LZ4_ra(119.2%), 156B }  # Key variables in the GDS: # - sample.id: individual identifiers # - snp.chromosome / snp.position / snp.id / snp.ref / snp.alt: SNP metadata # - dosage/anc0, dosage/anc1, ...: ancestry-specific risk-allele dosage # - hapcount/anc0, hapcount/anc1, ...: ancestry-specific haplotype count  # List top-level nodes gdsfmt::ls.gdsn(gds) #> [1] "sample.id"      "snp.chromosome" "snp.position"   "snp.id"         #> [5] "snp.ref"        "snp.alt"        "dosage"         "hapcount"  # Close the GDS connection gdsfmt::closefn.gds(gds) # Open the GDS file in read-only mode gds <- gdsfmt::openfn.gds(gds_path, readonly = TRUE)  # Read sample IDs sample_id <- gdsfmt::read.gdsn(gdsfmt::index.gdsn(gds, "sample.id"))  # Read SNP metadata snp_meta <- data.frame(   snp.id = gdsfmt::read.gdsn(gdsfmt::index.gdsn(gds, "snp.id")),   snp.chromosome = gdsfmt::read.gdsn(gdsfmt::index.gdsn(gds, "snp.chromosome")),   snp.position = gdsfmt::read.gdsn(gdsfmt::index.gdsn(gds, "snp.position")),   snp.ref = gdsfmt::read.gdsn(gdsfmt::index.gdsn(gds, "snp.ref")),   snp.alt = gdsfmt::read.gdsn(gdsfmt::index.gdsn(gds, "snp.alt")),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # Access ancestry-specific dosage/hapcount nodes dosage_anc0_node <- gdsfmt::index.gdsn(gds, "dosage/anc0") hapcount_anc0_node <- gdsfmt::index.gdsn(gds, "hapcount/anc0")  # Check dimensions (should be n_samples x n_snps) dosage_anc0_dim <- gdsfmt::objdesp.gdsn(dosage_anc0_node)$dim hapcount_anc0_dim <- gdsfmt::objdesp.gdsn(hapcount_anc0_node)$dim  # Set safe example indices n_sample <- length(sample_id) n_snp <- nrow(snp_meta) snp_idx <- min(2L, n_snp) sample_idx <- min(5L, n_sample)  # 1) All samples for one SNP in ancestry 0 dosage_anc0_snp <- gdsfmt::read.gdsn(dosage_anc0_node,                                       start = c(1L, snp_idx),                                       count = c(n_sample, 1L)                                      ) hapcount_anc0_snp <- gdsfmt::read.gdsn(hapcount_anc0_node,                                         start = c(1L, snp_idx),                                         count = c(n_sample, 1L)                                        )  # 2) All SNPs for one individual in ancestry 0 dosage_anc0_sample <- gdsfmt::read.gdsn(dosage_anc0_node,                                         start = c(sample_idx, 1L),                                          count = c(1L, n_snp)                                         ) hapcount_anc0_sample <- gdsfmt::read.gdsn(hapcount_anc0_node,                                            start = c(sample_idx, 1L),                                            count = c(1L, n_snp)                                           )  # 3) One sample x one SNP in ancestry 0 dosage_anc0_cell <- gdsfmt::read.gdsn(dosage_anc0_node,                                        start = c(sample_idx, snp_idx),                                       count = c(1L, 1L)                                       ) hapcount_anc0_cell <- gdsfmt::read.gdsn(hapcount_anc0_node,                                          start = c(sample_idx, snp_idx),                                          count = c(1L, 1L)                                         )  # Access another ancestry by changing the node path, e.g. "dosage/anc1" dosage_anc1_node <- gdsfmt::index.gdsn(gds, "dosage/anc1")  # Close the GDS connection gdsfmt::closefn.gds(gds)

previous_headings: Step 1: Extract tracts and convert formats
title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: extract_tracts() extract_tracts_flare() functions support multiple output formats via output_formats argument. Though GDS format required downstream TLS-Tractor steps, can optionally export text VCF formats inspection, sharing, use external tools. "gds" (default): Genomic Data Structure format—binary, compressed, optimized fast access scalability (required downstream TLS-Tractor steps). "txt" "txt.gz": Plain-text gzip-compressed text format. ancestry, two files created: <prefix>.anc{k}.dosage.txt(.gz) <prefix>.anc{k}.hapcount.txt(.gz), <prefix> basename input VCF file vcf_path without .vcf .vcf.gz. former contains number copies risk allele ancestry, latter contains number haplotypes ancestry. Output files columns CHROM POS ID REF ALT followed one column per sample. "vcf" "vcf.gz": VCF format. ancestry, one file created: <prefix>.anc{k}.vcf(.gz) FORMAT=GT, <prefix> basename input VCF file vcf_path without .vcf .vcf.gz. Haplotypes assigned ancestry written .. can request multiple formats single run passing vector (e.g., output_formats = c("gds", "txt.gz", "vcf.gz")). Note request compressed uncompressed versions format (e.g., "txt" "txt.gz"), compressed version written disk save space. Example: extract GDS compressed text one call using FLARE VCF input.
code: extract_tracts_flare(   vcf_path = file.path(data_dir, "simulate_linear.flare.vcf"),   num_ancs = 2L,   output_dir = output_dir_tracts,   output_formats = c("gds", "txt.gz")  # Multiple formats in one call )  # Outputs will be in output_dir_tracts/ with the filenames: # - simulate_linear.flare.gds (GDS format) # - simulate_linear.flare.anc0.dosage.txt.gz (dosage for ancestry 0) # - simulate_linear.flare.anc0.hapcount.txt.gz (hapcount for ancestry 0) # - simulate_linear.flare.anc1.dosage.txt.gz (dosage for ancestry 1) # - simulate_linear.flare.anc1.hapcount.txt.gz (hapcount for ancestry 1)

previous_headings: Step 1: Extract tracts and convert formats
title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: extracting tracts, can convert GDS text formats using following functions: input_prefix: prefix input text files, without .anc{k}.dosage.txt(.gz) .anc{k}.hapcount.txt(.gz) num_ancs: number ancestral populations dataset output_prefix: prefix output .gds file, without extension (NULL uses input_prefix) chunk_size: number variants processed per chunk (default: 1024L). Larger values can improve speed increase memory usage. GDS output <output_prefix>.gds. gds_path: input GDS file path output_prefix: prefix output text files (NULL uses input GDS filename prefix directory) output_format: output text format, either "txt" "txt.gz" (default: "txt.gz") chunk_size: number variants processed per chunk (default: 1024L). Larger values can improve speed increase memory usage. output include two files per ancestry: <output_prefix>.anc{k}.dosage.txt(.gz) <output_prefix>.anc{k}.hapcount.txt(.gz).
code: # TXT (or txt.gz) -> GDS convert_txt_to_gds(   input_prefix = file.path(output_dir_tracts, "simulate_linear.flare"),   num_ancs = 2L,   output_prefix = file.path(output_dir_tracts, "simulate_linear_from_txt") ) # Output: output_dir_tracts/simulate_linear_from_txt.gds  # GDS -> TXT (or txt.gz) convert_gds_to_txt(   gds_path = file.path(output_dir_tracts, "simulate_linear.flare.gds"),   output_prefix = file.path(output_dir_tracts, "simulate_linear_from_gds"),   output_format = "txt.gz" ) # Outputs will be in output_dir_tracts/ with the filenames: # - simulate_linear_from_gds.anc0.dosage.txt.gz # - simulate_linear_from_gds.anc0.hapcount.txt.gz # - simulate_linear_from_gds.anc1.dosage.txt.gz # - simulate_linear_from_gds.anc1.hapcount.txt.gz

previous_headings: Step 1: Extract tracts and convert formats
title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: Use ? inspect documentation quickly.
code: ?tlstractor::extract_tracts ?tlstractor::extract_tracts_flare ?tlstractor::convert_txt_to_gds ?tlstractor::convert_gds_to_txt

what: Step 2: Munge external GWAS summary statistics
title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: creating GDS file individual-level data main/internal study, use munge_sumstats() harmonize external GWAS summary statistics GDS file. function performs quality control (QC), aligns variants summary statistics GDS file using either CHR-POS ID, outputs munged summary statistics restricted SNPs present datasets. Expected input format GWAS summary statistics: input file tab-delimited. Required columns match_by = "CHR-POS": chromosome, position, reference allele, alternative allele, effect size, standard error (default column names: CHR, POS, REF, ALT, BETA, SE). Required columns match_by = "ID": variant ID, reference allele, alternative allele, effect size, standard error (default column names: ID, REF, ALT, BETA, SE). Optional column: allele frequency (default AF), available. Additional columns allowed ignored munging process. match_by = "CHR-POS", accepted chromosome formats autosomes : 1-22, 01-22, chr1-chr22, chr01-chr22 (case-insensitive). BETA column represent effect size estimate alternative allele (log odds ratio logistic regression linear coefficient linear regression), SE column contain corresponding standard error. column names summary statistics file differ defaults, can specify column names using function arguments: chr_col, pos_col, id_col, ref_col, alt_col, beta_col, se_col, af_col. QC performed munge_sumstats() GWAS summary statistics (applied order): REF/ALT checks: keep biallelic SNPs single-nucleotide /C/G/T alleles remove rest optional removal ambiguous SNPs (/T, T/, C/G, G/C) autosome restriction chromosomes 1-22 match_by = "CHR-POS" POS > 0 match_by = "CHR-POS" non-missing non-empty ID match_by = "ID" duplicate removal key: unique (CHR, POS) match_by = "CHR-POS", unique ID match_by = "ID" effect/SE checks: BETA != NA SE > 0 optional AF range check (0 < AF < 1) AF provided allele flipping needed align GDS reference allele definition Output format munged summary statistics: output file tab-delimited. Output columns standardized CHR, POS, ID, REF, ALT, BETA, SE, AF (optional), GDS_ID. output aligned GDS metadata (chromosome, position, variant ID, alleles) variant definitions consistent GDS file. GDS_ID 1-based variant index GDS file match variants summary statistics variants GDS file. used tlstractor() Step 3. example call : gds_path: path GDS file generated Step 1 sumstats_path: path external GWAS summary statistics file match_by: method align variants summary statistics GDS file, either "CHR-POS" (default) "ID" remove_ambiguous: whether remove ambiguous SNPs (default: TRUE) output_path: output path munged summary statistics (NULL uses <sumstats_prefix>_munged.txt directory sumstats_path, <sumstats_prefix> input summary statistics filename without extension) Preview munged summary statistics. Use ? inspect full documentation.
code: # Create output directory for munged summary statistics output_dir_munge <- file.path(tempdir(),                                "tlstractor_tutorial",                               "linear", "munged_sumstats") dir.create(output_dir_munge, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  # Define output path for munged summary statistics munged_sumstats_path <- file.path(output_dir_munge,                                   "external_gwas_sumstats_munged.txt")  # Munge summary statistics munge_sumstats(   gds_path = gds_path,   sumstats_path = file.path(data_dir,                             "simulate_linear.external_gwas_sumstats.txt"),   match_by = "CHR-POS",   remove_ambiguous = TRUE,   output_path = munged_sumstats_path ) #> 0 rows excluded: invalid alleles (not biallelic SNPs). #> 0 rows excluded: ambiguous SNPs (A/T, T/A, C/G, G/C). #> 0 rows excluded: non-autosomal or invalid chromosomes. #> 0 rows excluded: invalid POS values. #> 0 rows excluded: duplicate (CHR, POS) pairs. #> 0 rows excluded: invalid BETA or SE values. #> 0 rows excluded: invalid AF values. #> Variants retained after QC and matching: 5 #> Munged summary statistics written to: /tmp/RtmpveE2wN/tlstractor_tutorial/linear/munged_sumstats/external_gwas_sumstats_munged.txt munged_sumstats_preview <- utils::read.delim(   munged_sumstats_path,   header = TRUE,   sep = "\t",   check.names = FALSE,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) head(munged_sumstats_preview) #>    CHR  POS        ID REF ALT         BETA         SE        AF GDS_ID #> 1 chr1 1000 rs0000001   A   G  0.065739730 0.01338672 0.2983750      1 #> 2 chr1 1010 rs0000002   A   G  0.051836263 0.01287591 0.3506250      2 #> 3 chr1 1020 rs0000003   A   G -0.004204495 0.01285285 0.3522500      3 #> 4 chr1 1030 rs0000004   A   G  0.038744761 0.01246723 0.3762500      4 #> 5 chr1 1040 rs0000005   A   G  0.059339484 0.01267735 0.3783125      5 ?tlstractor::munge_sumstats

title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: individual-level GDS file phenotype/covariates internal study, munged external GWAS summary statistics, ready run TLS-Tractor. TLS-Tractor estimates ancestry-specific genetic effects optional local-ancestry adjustment. key advantage although external summary statistics come standard GWAS models, TLS-Tractor strategically leverages via transfer learning obtain unbiased efficient estimates ancestry-specific parameters. Model assumptions TLS-Tractor: - Unrelated individuals internal cohort - sample overlap internal external cohorts - Transportability assumption: comparable admixture profiles across cohorts (e.g., similar ancestral populations proportions). assumption testable practice, concrete diagnostic workflow provided Assumption checking subsection . Looking ahead, relax assumption, next version TLS-Tractor planned support settings internal external cohorts share ancestral populations different ancestry proportions.

title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: gds_path: Path GDS file generated Step 1, containing local ancestry risk allele dosage information internal cohort. sumstats_path: Path munged external GWAS summary statistics file generated Step 2, must harmonized aligned GDS file. method: Choose "linear" continuous phenotypes "logistic" binary phenotypes. cond_local: Set TRUE condition local-ancestry terms, recommended admixed population studies. keeps interpretation ancestry-specific SNP effects consistent interpretation marginal effect single-ancestry GWAS. Set FALSE estimate ancestry-specific SNP effects without local-ancestry adjustment, can increase power may susceptible confounding local ancestry. pheno_path, pheno_id_col, pheno_col: Specify phenotype file, along column containing sample IDs column containing phenotype values. Sample IDs must match GDS file phenotype file, intersecting samples used. covar_path, covar_id_col, covar_cols: Optionally provide covariate file, column containing sample IDs, vector columns include covariates (e.g., PCs, age, sex). covariates supplied, sample IDs must match GDS file covariate files, intersecting samples used. covariates needed, set covar_path = NULL. output_prefix: Output path prefix result files. TLS-Tractor writes main result <output_prefix>.txt.gz may write <output_prefix>.excluded_samples.txt samples dropped GDS file intersection/filtering. scratch_dir: Optional directory temporary per-task files merge. NULL (default), run-specific temporary directory created dirname(output_prefix) name <basename(output_prefix)>_<pid>_<YYYYmmdd_HHMMSS>_tmp. Temporary files deleted upon successful completion. directory removed created function. snp_start: 1-based starting SNP index GDS file (default 1L). Useful analyzing subset SNPs. snp_count: Number SNPs process snp_start. NULL (default), analysis continues end GDS file. n_cores: Number CPU cores use parallelization (default 1L). Increase speed runtime require memory. chunk_size: Number variants processed per chunk per core (default 1024L). Larger values can improve speed increase memory usage. local_ancestry_mac_threshold: Ancestry-specific minor-allele count threshold (default 20L). SNPs ancestry-specific MAC threshold skipped returned NA inferential columns. use_fast_version: Defaults TRUE (recommended, especially large datasets). fast mode, TLS-Tractor assumes estimated non-genetic covariate effects null model (phenotype ~ covariates) close full standard GWAS model (phenotype ~ SNP dosage + covariates) single SNP, estimated covariate effects null model can reused across variants reduce computation. fast mode substantially faster little loss accuracy. Set FALSE force full per-variant fitting want maximum robustness. covariates provided, fast mode automatically disabled. Run TLS-Tractor using internal individual-level data munged external summary statistics:
code: # Create output directory for TLS-Tractor results output_dir_results <- file.path(tempdir(), "tlstractor_tutorial",                                 "linear", "results") dir.create(output_dir_results, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  # Run TLS-Tractor tlstractor(   gds_path = gds_path,   sumstats_path = munged_sumstats_path,   method = "linear",   cond_local = TRUE,   pheno_path = file.path(data_dir, "simulate_linear.pheno.txt"),   pheno_id_col = "id",   pheno_col = "y",   covar_path = file.path(data_dir, "simulate_linear.covar.txt"),   covar_id_col = "id",   covar_cols = c("cov1", "cov2", "cov3"),   output_prefix = file.path(output_dir_results, "tlstractor_linear") ) #> Reading GDS metadata... #> Total samples in GDS: 100 #> Total SNPs in GDS: 5 #> Loading phenotype data... #> Loading covariate data... #> Filtering samples... #> Samples after filtering: 100 #> Preprocessing data... #> Loading summary statistics... #> Adjusting chunk_size from 1024 to 5 to ensure enough tasks for 1 cores. #> Parallel setup: using 1 of 4 available cores. Planned 1 tasks to process SNPs [1, 5] (5 total). Each task handles up to 5 SNPs (last task may be smaller). Genotypes are read in chunks of 5 SNPs within each task. #> Scratch directory: /tmp/RtmpveE2wN/tlstractor_tutorial/linear/results/tlstractor_linear_14630_20260330_000632_tmp #> Output filepath: /tmp/RtmpveE2wN/tlstractor_tutorial/linear/results/tlstractor_linear.txt.gz #> Initializing parallel cluster... #> Running TLS-Tractor analysis in parallel... #> Merging results... #> Cleaning up temporary files... #> TLS-Tractor analysis complete! #> Results written to: /tmp/RtmpveE2wN/tlstractor_tutorial/linear/results/tlstractor_linear.txt.gz

title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: TLS-Tractor outputs summary statistics file <output_prefix>.txt.gz following columns: CHROM: Chromosome POS: Base-pair position ID: Variant ID REF: Reference allele ALT: Alternate allele main_N: Number samples analyzed internal/main study AF: Overall allele frequency (internal study) AF_anc*: Ancestry-specific allele frequency ancestry (e.g., AF_anc0, AF_anc1 two-way admixture) LAprop_anc*: Local ancestry proportion ancestry has_sumstats: Logical indicator whether external summary statistics available variant. Variants without summary statistics analyzed using internal data alone. fallback_used: Logical indicator whether QR decomposition fallback used estimation; TRUE, results may reduced numerical stability interpreted caution joint_pval: Joint p-value testing ancestry-specific SNP dosage effects beta_anc*: Effect size (log odds logistic, linear coefficient linear) ancestry se_anc*: Standard error effect size ancestry pval_anc*: P-value ancestry-specific effect LAeff_anc*: Effect size (log odds logistic, linear coefficient linear) local ancestry term ancestry LAse_anc*: Standard error local ancestry effect ancestry LApval_anc*: P-value local ancestry effect ancestry

title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: practical way assess TLS-Tractor’s transportability assumption test effect heterogeneity internal external cohorts. Specifically, - internal cohort, obtain effect estimates β1\beta_1 standard errors SE1SE_1 SNP standard GWAS model (fitted using tools PLINK2) - external GWAS summary statistics, obtain effect estimates β2\beta_2 standard errors SE2SE_2 SNPs (already available munged summary statistics file) SNP, test whether effect sizes consistent across cohorts using: z=β1−β2SE12+SE22 z = \frac{\beta_1 - \beta_2}{\sqrt{SE_1^2 + SE_2^2}} null hypothesis H0:β1=β2H_0: \beta_1 = \beta_2, zz statistic approximately follows standard normal distribution, assuming two estimates independent. two-sided p-value can computed accordingly. Wald test heterogeneity two independent estimates, equivalent Cochran’s Q test special case two studies (.e., Q=z2Q = z^2). computing p-values genome-wide, QQ plot can used assess calibration heterogeneity test. transportability assumption satisfied, SNPs exhibit evidence heterogeneity, observed p-values follow expected null distribution (.e., align diagonal). Systematic deviations may indicate violations assumption.
code: # Example workflow: # 1) Run standard GWAS in the internal cohort and obtain per-SNP effect size #    (beta_1) and standard error (se_1). # 2) Merge with effect size (beta_2) and standard error (se_2) from external #    GWAS summary statistics by SNP ID (or CHR/POS/alleles).  assump_dt <- merge(   internal_gwas[, c("ID", "beta_1", "se_1")],   external_sumstats[, c("ID", "beta_2", "se_2")],   by = "ID" )  # Wald z-test for equality of two independent estimates assump_dt$z <- (assump_dt$beta_1 - assump_dt$beta_2) /   sqrt(assump_dt$se_1^2 + assump_dt$se_2^2) assump_dt$pval <- 2 * stats::pnorm(-abs(assump_dt$z))  # QQ plot of p-values p_sorted <- sort(assump_dt$pval) n <- length(p_sorted) exp_logp <- -log10(stats::ppoints(n)) obs_logp <- -log10(p_sorted)  # 95% CI under the null (order-statistic based) idx <- seq_len(n) ci_low <- stats::qbeta(0.025, idx, n - idx + 1) ci_high <- stats::qbeta(0.975, idx, n - idx + 1) ci_lower_logp <- -log10(pmax(ci_high, .Machine$double.xmin)) ci_upper_logp <- -log10(pmax(ci_low, .Machine$double.xmin))  plot(   exp_logp,   obs_logp,   type = "n",   xlab = expression(Expected~ -log[10](p)),   ylab = expression(Observed~ -log[10](p)),   main = "QQ Plot of Heterogeneity Test P-values" )  polygon(   c(exp_logp, rev(exp_logp)),   c(ci_lower_logp, rev(ci_upper_logp)),   col = grDevices::adjustcolor("grey70", alpha.f = 0.35),   border = NA )  points(exp_logp, obs_logp, pch = 20, cex = 0.6) abline(0, 1, col = "red", lwd = 2)

title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: Use ? inspect full documentation.
code: ?tlstractor::tlstractor

title: TLS-Tractor Tutorial
text: code chunk illustrates full workflow simulated binary data, tract extraction final TLS-Tractor output.
code: # Get path to tutorial data included in the package data_dir <- system.file("extdata", package = "tlstractor")  # Store all temporary outputs under tempdir()/tlstractor_tutorial/binary/ work_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "tlstractor_tutorial", "binary") dir.create(work_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  # 1) Extract tracts to GDS (choose one extractor; this example uses FLARE input) extract_tracts_flare(   vcf_path = file.path(data_dir, "simulate_binary.flare.vcf"),   num_ancs = 2L,   output_dir = work_dir,   output_formats = "gds" ) gds_path <- file.path(work_dir, "simulate_binary.flare.gds")  # If using RFMix2/Gnomix inputs instead, use: # extract_tracts( #   vcf_path = file.path(data_dir, "simulate_binary.phased.vcf"), #   msp_path = file.path(data_dir, "simulate_binary.msp.tsv"), #   num_ancs = 2L, #   output_dir = work_dir, #   output_formats = "gds" # ) # gds_path <- file.path(work_dir, "simulate_binary.phased.gds")  # 2) Munge external GWAS summary statistics munged_sumstats_path <- file.path(work_dir, "external_gwas_sumstats_munged.txt") munge_sumstats(   gds_path = gds_path,   sumstats_path = file.path(data_dir,                             "simulate_binary.external_gwas_sumstats.txt"),   match_by = "CHR-POS",   remove_ambiguous = TRUE,   output_path = munged_sumstats_path )  # 3) Run TLS-Tractor tlstractor(   gds_path = gds_path,   sumstats_path = munged_sumstats_path,   method = "logistic",   cond_local = TRUE,   pheno_path = file.path(data_dir, "simulate_binary.pheno.txt"),   pheno_id_col = "id",   pheno_col = "y",   covar_path = file.path(data_dir, "simulate_binary.covar.txt"),   covar_id_col = "id",   covar_cols = c("cov1", "cov2", "cov3"),   output_prefix = file.path(work_dir, "tlstractor_binary") )

what: Transfer Learning of Summary Statistics to Tractor
title: Transfer Learning of Summary Statistics to Tractor
text: R package tlstractor implements TLS-Tractor (Transfer Learning Summary Statistics Tractor), transfer learning framework improve power local ancestry-aware genome-wide association studies (GWAS) admixed populations. common practical challenge local ancestry-aware GWAS limited sample size. Standard GWAS can scale meta-analyzing summary statistics across cohorts, local ancestry-aware analyses typically require individual-level data directly use existing summary statistics cohorts. TLS-Tractor addresses gap combining internal individual-level data external GWAS summary statistics estimate ancestry-specific genetic effects, optional adjustment local ancestry. Core computational steps implemented C++ keep analyses efficient scalable large datasets. method assumes unrelated individuals internal cohort, sample overlap internal external cohorts, comparable admixture profiles across cohorts. Last updated: March 29, 2026 Current version: 0.0.0.9000 (development)

title: Transfer Learning of Summary Statistics to Tractor
text: Installation Quick start Installation notes Bug reports License

title: Transfer Learning of Summary Statistics to Tractor

code: install.packages("pak") pak::pkg_install("Wenxuan-Lu/tlstractor")

title: Transfer Learning of Summary Statistics to Tractor
text: Use option fixed, reproducible version. installation fails want build vignettes locally, see Installation notes. Back Contents
code: install.packages("pak") pak::pkg_install("https://github.com/Wenxuan-Lu/tlstractor/releases/download/vX.Y.Z/tlstractor_X.Y.Z.tar.gz")

title: Transfer Learning of Summary Statistics to Tractor
text: TLS-Tractor extends Tractor incorporating external GWAS summary statistics local ancestry-aware association analysis. background GWAS admixed populations local ancestry inference, see Tractor tutorial. package follows 3-step workflow: extract local-ancestry tracts, harmonize external GWAS summary statistics, combine two data sources run local ancestry-aware association analysis. implementation first step (extract_tracts() / extract_tracts_flare()) workflow-compatible Tractor. designed drop-replacement implemented C++ substantially faster tract extraction. workflows need tract files different formats, provide functions convert_gds_to_txt() convert_txt_to_gds() quick format conversion. provide minimal runnable script illustration. full pipeline walkthrough, see TLS-Tractor tutorial. Required inputs: Individual-level data main study / internal cohort: Phased genotypes local ancestry inference results (choose one): using RFMix2/Gnomix LAI: cohort.vcf(.gz) + cohort.msp.tsv(.gz) cohort.vcf(.gz): phased genotypes (GT, e.g. 0|1) cohort.msp.tsv(.gz): local ancestry labels using FLARE LAI: cohort.vcf(.gz) FLARE output VCF containing phased genotypes local ancestry labels Phenotype file: pheno.txt columns id, pheno Optional covariates: covariates.txt columns id, cov1, cov2, cov3 Summary-level data external cohort: External GWAS summary statistics (standard GWAS model): external_sumstats.txt columns CHR, POS, ID, REF, ALT, BETA, SE, optional column AF. Filenames column names illustrative; map data schema. Main outputs: Tracts GDS (cohort.gds) — HDF5-based GDS file containing: sample.id — sample identifiers snp.chromosome, snp.position, snp.id, snp.ref, snp.alt — variant metadata dosage/anc0..ancK, hapcount/anc0..ancK — ancestry-specific dosage (0, 1, 2) local ancestry haplotype count (0, 1, 2) matrices Munged summary statistics (external_sumstats_munged.txt) — Tab-delimited file columns: CHR, POS, ID, REF, ALT — variant information BETA, SE — effect size standard error AF (optional) — allele frequency available GDS_ID — internal index linking GDS file variant TLS-Tractor results (tlstractor.txt.gz) — Gzip-compressed tab-delimited file output columns organized follows: Variant metadata: CHROM, POS, ID, REF, ALT — Variant information main_N — Number samples analyzed internal/main study Allele frequency ancestry composition: AF — Overall allele frequency (calculated internal cohort) AF_anc* — Ancestry-specific allele frequency ancestry (e.g., AF_anc0, AF_anc1 two-way admixture) LAprop_anc* — Proportion local ancestry ancestry (reflects ancestry makeup locus) Analysis metadata: has_sumstats — Logical indicator (TRUE/FALSE) whether external summary statistics available variant. FALSE, variant analyzed using internal data alone fallback_used — Logical indicator (TRUE/FALSE) whether QR decomposition fallback used statistical estimation. TRUE, results may reduced numerical stability interpreted cautiously Association results (main output): joint_pval — Joint p-value testing ancestry-specific SNP dosage effects beta_anc* — Effect size (linear coefficient linear phenotype, log odds logistic phenotype) ancestry se_anc* — Standard error ancestry-specific effect size pval_anc* — P-value ancestry-specific effect Local ancestry effects (present cond_local=TRUE): LAeff_anc* — Effect size local ancestry term ancestry LAse_anc* — Standard error local ancestry effect LApval_anc* — P-value local ancestry effect Next: Full pipeline walkthrough: TLS-Tractor tutorial Tutorial statistical phasing local ancestry inference: Tractor tutorial Function help R: ?extract_tracts, ?extract_tracts_flare, ?convert_gds_to_txt, ?convert_txt_to_gds, ?munge_sumstats, ?tlstractor Back Contents
code: library(tlstractor)  # 1) Extract tracts (choose one option)  # Option A: phased VCF + MSP (RFMix2/Gnomix) extract_tracts(   vcf_path = "cohort.vcf.gz",   msp_path = "cohort.msp.tsv",   num_ancs = 2L,   output_formats = "gds" )  # Option B: FLARE VCF # extract_tracts_flare( #   vcf_path = "cohort.vcf.gz", #   num_ancs = 2L, #   output_formats = "gds" # )  # 2) QC + align external summary statistics munge_sumstats(   gds_path = "cohort.gds",   sumstats_path = "external_sumstats.txt",   match_by = "CHR-POS",   output_path = "external_sumstats_munged.txt" )  # 3) Run TLS-Tractor tlstractor(   gds_path = "cohort.gds",   sumstats_path = "external_sumstats_munged.txt",   method = "linear", # or "logistic"   cond_local = TRUE, # whether to condition on local ancestry terms   pheno_path = "pheno.txt",   pheno_id_col = "id",   pheno_col = "pheno",   covar_path = "covariates.txt",   covar_id_col = "id",   covar_cols = c("cov1", "cov2", "cov3"),   output_prefix = "tlstractor",   n_cores = 4L )

what: Convert Local-Ancestry Tracts Format from GDS to Text — convert_gds_to_txt

text: Converts ancestry-specific dosage hapcount matrices stored tracts GDS file tab-delimited text files (plain gzipped).

code: convert_gds_to_txt(   gds_path,   output_prefix = NULL,   output_format = "txt.gz",   chunk_size = 1024L )

title: Convert Local-Ancestry Tracts Format from GDS to Text — convert_gds_to_txt
text: gds_path Character; input tracts GDS path. output_prefix Character NULL; output file prefix. NULL (default), uses input filename prefix directory gds_path. output_format Character; output format, one "txt" "txt.gz" (default). chunk_size Integer; number variants processed per chunk. Default 1024; higher values can improve speed increase memory usage.

title: Convert Local-Ancestry Tracts Format from GDS to Text — convert_gds_to_txt
text: Invisibly returns NULL. ancestry k, writes two files: <output_prefix>.anc{k}.dosage.txt(.gz) <output_prefix>.anc{k}.hapcount.txt(.gz).

title: Convert Local-Ancestry Tracts Format from GDS to Text — convert_gds_to_txt
text: Output tables contain columns CHROM, POS, ID, REF, ALT, followed one column per sample sample.id input GDS.

title: Convert Local-Ancestry Tracts Format from Text to GDS — convert_txt_to_gds

code: convert_txt_to_gds(   input_prefix,   num_ancs,   output_prefix = NULL,   chunk_size = 1024L )

title: Convert Local-Ancestry Tracts Format from Text to GDS — convert_txt_to_gds
text: input_prefix Character; input file prefix used locate ancestry files. Expected files per ancestry k: <input_prefix>.anc{k}.dosage.txt(.gz) <input_prefix>.anc{k}.hapcount.txt(.gz). num_ancs Integer; number ancestral populations (must > 1). output_prefix Character NULL; output GDS prefix. NULL (default), uses input_prefix. chunk_size Integer; number variants processed per chunk. Default 1024; higher values can improve speed increase memory usage.

text: generated GDS contains sample.id, variant metadata (snp.chromosome, snp.position, snp.id, snp.ref, snp.alt), ancestry-specific nodes dosage/anc0..ancK hapcount/anc0..ancK.

what: Extract Local-Ancestry Tracts from Phased VCF and MSP (RFMix2/Gnomix) — extract_tracts
title: Extract Local-Ancestry Tracts from Phased VCF and MSP (RFMix2/Gnomix) — extract_tracts
text: Parses phased VCF together MSP local-ancestry file writes ancestry-specific outputs one formats: gds, txt, txt.gz, vcf, vcf.gz.

title: Extract Local-Ancestry Tracts from Phased VCF and MSP (RFMix2/Gnomix) — extract_tracts
code: extract_tracts(   vcf_path,   msp_path,   num_ancs,   output_dir = NULL,   output_formats = "gds",   chunk_size = 1024L )

title: Extract Local-Ancestry Tracts from Phased VCF and MSP (RFMix2/Gnomix) — extract_tracts
text: vcf_path Character; input phased VCF path (.vcf .vcf.gz). msp_path Character; input MSP path. num_ancs Integer; number ancestral populations (must > 1). output_dir Character NULL; output directory (must exist). NULL (default), uses directory vcf_path. output_formats Character vector; output format specification (example "gds" c("gds", "txt.gz")). Default "gds". Supported values: gds, txt, txt.gz, vcf, vcf.gz. compressed uncompressed versions requested type, compressed output written: txt.gz txt, vcf.gz vcf. chunk_size Integer; number variants processed per chunk. Default 1024; higher values can improve speed increase memory usage.

title: Extract Local-Ancestry Tracts from Phased VCF and MSP (RFMix2/Gnomix) — extract_tracts
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Output files written filename prefix derived vcf_path output_dir.

title: Extract Local-Ancestry Tracts from Phased VCF and MSP (RFMix2/Gnomix) — extract_tracts
text: Output files (prefix = basename vcf_path without .vcf .vcf.gz): gds: one file <prefix>.gds containing sample.id, variant metadata (snp.chromosome, snp.position, snp.id, snp.ref, snp.alt), ancestry-specific nodes dosage/anc0..ancK hapcount/anc0..ancK. txt / txt.gz: ancestry k, two files <prefix>.anc{k}.dosage.txt(.gz) <prefix>.anc{k}.hapcount.txt(.gz). Columns CHROM POS ID REF ALT followed one column per sample. vcf / vcf.gz: ancestry k, one file <prefix>.anc{k}.vcf(.gz) FORMAT=GT; haplotypes assigned ancestry k written .. Assumptions inputs: Biallelic variants. missing genotype/local-ancestry fields. duplicate variants. Autosomal chromosomes (1-22, 01-22, chr1-chr22, chr01-chr22; case-insensitive). Alleles uppercase /C/G/T. GT first sample subfield (GT:...). GT phased (0|0, 0|1, 1|0, 1|1) single-digit allele codes. One chromosome per VCF per MSP file. Sample order consistent VCF MSP files.

title: Extract Local-Ancestry Tracts from a FLARE VCF file — extract_tracts_flare
text: vcf_path Character; input FLARE VCF path (.vcf .vcf.gz). num_ancs Integer; number ancestral populations (must > 1). output_dir Character NULL; output directory (must exist). NULL (default), uses directory vcf_path. output_formats Character vector; output format specification (example "gds" c("gds", "txt.gz")). Default "gds". Supported values: gds, txt, txt.gz, vcf, vcf.gz. compressed uncompressed versions requested type, compressed output written: txt.gz txt, vcf.gz vcf. chunk_size Integer; number variants processed per chunk. Default 1024; higher values can improve speed increase memory usage.

title: Extract Local-Ancestry Tracts from a FLARE VCF file — extract_tracts_flare
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Output files written filename prefix derived vcf_path output_dir.

title: Extract Local-Ancestry Tracts from a FLARE VCF file — extract_tracts_flare
text: Output files (prefix = basename vcf_path without .vcf .vcf.gz): gds: one file <prefix>.gds containing sample.id, variant metadata (snp.chromosome, snp.position, snp.id, snp.ref, snp.alt), ancestry-specific nodes dosage/anc0..ancK hapcount/anc0..ancK. txt / txt.gz: ancestry k, two files <prefix>.anc{k}.dosage.txt(.gz) <prefix>.anc{k}.hapcount.txt(.gz). Columns CHROM POS ID REF ALT followed one column per sample. vcf / vcf.gz: ancestry k, one file <prefix>.anc{k}.vcf(.gz) FORMAT=GT; haplotypes assigned ancestry k written .. Assumptions input FLARE VCF: Biallelic variants. missing genotype/local-ancestry fields. duplicate variants. Autosomal chromosomes (1-22, 01-22, chr1-chr22, chr01-chr22; case-insensitive). Alleles uppercase /C/G/T. Sample FORMAT exactly GT:AN1:AN2. GT phased (0|0, 0|1, 1|0, 1|1) single-digit allele codes.

what: Munge GWAS Summary Statistics to Align with GDS File — munge_sumstats
title: Munge GWAS Summary Statistics to Align with GDS File — munge_sumstats
text: Aligns GWAS summary statistics GDS file using chromosome-position variant ID matching. Performs quality control filtering, allele flipping discordant variants, outputs filtered results.

title: Munge GWAS Summary Statistics to Align with GDS File — munge_sumstats
code: munge_sumstats(   gds_path,   sumstats_path,   match_by = "CHR-POS",   chr_col = "CHR",   pos_col = "POS",   id_col = "ID",   ref_col = "REF",   alt_col = "ALT",   beta_col = "BETA",   se_col = "SE",   af_col = "AF",   remove_ambiguous = TRUE,   output_path = NULL )

title: Munge GWAS Summary Statistics to Align with GDS File — munge_sumstats
text: gds_path Character; path input GDS file. sumstats_path Character; path GWAS summary statistics file. match_by Character; "CHR-POS" (default) "ID". "CHR-POS": match chromosome position (autosomes 1-22 ). "ID": match variant ID. chr_col Character; chromosome column name GWAS summary statistics file (default "CHR"). pos_col Character; position column name GWAS summary statistics file (default "POS"). id_col Character; variant ID column name GWAS summary statistics file (default "ID"). ref_col Character; reference allele column name GWAS summary statistics file (default "REF"). alt_col Character; alternative allele column name GWAS summary statistics file (default "ALT"). beta_col Character; effect size column name GWAS summary statistics file (default "BETA"). se_col Character; standard error column name GWAS summary statistics file (default "SE"). af_col Character; allele frequency column name GWAS summary statistics file (default "AF"). Optional; omitted output absent input GWAS summary statistics. remove_ambiguous Logical; TRUE (default), exclude ambiguous SNPs. output_path Character NULL; output file path. NULL (default), auto-generated <sumstats_prefix>_munged.txt directory sumstats_path, <sumstats_prefix> filename prefix sumstats_path without extension.

title: Munge GWAS Summary Statistics to Align with GDS File — munge_sumstats
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Filtered results written output_path tab-delimited text columns: CHR, POS, ID, REF, ALT, BETA, SE, AF (optional), GDS_ID. GDS_ID 1-based variant index input GDS file match variants summary statistics variants GDS file.

title: Munge GWAS Summary Statistics to Align with GDS File — munge_sumstats
text: Required columns: input summary statistics must contain columns REF, ALT, BETA, SE, along either (CHR POS) match_by = "CHR-POS" ID match_by = "ID". AF column optional. Additional columns allowed ignored. match_by = "CHR-POS", accepted chromosome formats autosomes : 1-22, 01-22, chr1-chr22, chr01-chr22 (case-insensitive). BETA column represent effect size estimate alternative allele (log odds ratio logistic regression linear coefficient linear regression), SE column contain corresponding standard error. Quality control filters summary statistics (applied order): REF/ALT: single /C/G/T nucleotides, biallelic Ambiguous SNPs: optionally remove /T, T/, C/G, G/C Autosomes: restrict chromosomes 1-22 (match_by = "CHR-POS") POS: POS > 0 (match_by = "CHR-POS") ID: non-missing non-empty (match_by = "ID") Duplicates: retain variants unique (CHR, POS) pairs (match_by = "CHR-POS") unique ID (match_by = "ID") Effect/SE: BETA != NA, SE > 0 AF range: 0 < AF < 1 (present)

title: Run TLS-Tractor — tlstractor
code: tlstractor(   gds_path,   sumstats_path,   method,   cond_local,   pheno_path,   pheno_id_col,   pheno_col,   covar_path = NULL,   covar_id_col = NULL,   covar_cols = NULL,   output_prefix,   scratch_dir = NULL,   snp_start = 1L,   snp_count = NULL,   n_cores = 1L,   chunk_size = 1024L,   local_ancestry_mac_threshold = 20L,   use_fast_version = TRUE )

title: Run TLS-Tractor — tlstractor
text: gds_path Character; path input GDS file. sumstats_path Character; path munged summary statistics file. method Character; one "linear" "logistic". cond_local Logical; whether condition local-ancestry dosage terms. pheno_path Character; path phenotype file. pheno_id_col Character; sample-ID column name phenotype file. pheno_col Character; phenotype column name phenotype file. covar_path Character NULL; path optional covariate file. Default NULL. covar_id_col Character NULL; sample-ID column name covariate file (required covar_path provided). Default NULL. covar_cols Character vector NULL; covariate column names covariate file (required covar_path provided). Default NULL. output_prefix Character; output path prefix. scratch_dir Character NULL; directory used temporarily store per-task result files merge. NULL, run-specific directory created dirname(output_prefix) name <basename(output_prefix)>_<pid>_<YYYYmmdd_HHMMSS>_tmp. Default NULL. snp_start Integer; 1-based starting SNP index GDS file. Default 1L. snp_count Integer NULL; number SNPs process. NULL, processing starts snp_start continues end GDS file. Default NULL. n_cores Integer; number CPU cores use. Default 1L. chunk_size Integer; number variants processed per chunk within CPU core. Default 1024; larger values may improve speed increase memory usage. local_ancestry_mac_threshold Integer; ancestry-specific minor-allele count threshold. SNPs ancestry-specific MAC threshold skipped. Skipped SNPs returned output NA values inferential columns. Default 20L. use_fast_version Logical; whether use fast TLS-Tractor mode. fast mode assumes estimated non-genetic covariate effects null model (phenotype ~ covariates) close full standard GWAS model (phenotype ~ SNP dosage + covariates) single SNP, estimated covariate effects null model can reused across variants reduce computation. fast mode can provide substantial speedup minimal loss accuracy. Default TRUE (recommended large datasets). Setting FALSE fources full per-variant fitting path, generally robust substantially slower covariates included. covariates provided, fast mode automatically disabled.

title: Run TLS-Tractor — tlstractor
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Writes gzipped GWAS results <output_prefix>.txt.gz. output includes: Variant metadata: CHROM, POS, ID, REF, ALT, main_N (sample size main/internal study) Frequency ancestry summaries: AF (overall allele frequency), AF_anc* (ancestry-specific allele frequency), LAprop_anc* (local-ancestry-specific haplotype proportion) Analysis metadata: has_sumstats (indicates whether external summary statistics available), fallback_used (indicates whether QR decomposition fallback used; TRUE, results may reduced numerical stability interpreted caution) Association results: joint_pval (p-value joint testing ancestry-specific SNP dosage effects), beta_anc*, se_anc*, pval_anc* (effect size, standard error, p-value ancestry-specific SNP dosage effect) cond_local = TRUE: LAeff_anc*, LAse_anc*, LApval_anc* (effect size, standard error, p-value local ancestry term) } Performs local ancestry-aware GWAS integrating individual-level data external GWAS summary statistics via transfer learning.   present, output file <output_prefix>.excluded_samples.txt containing sample IDs present GDS file excluded analysis sample intersection/filtering.execution, temporary per-task result files written scratch_dir/<run_tag>_task_<id>.txt.gz, run_tag run-specific identifier format tlstractor_<pid>_<YYYYmmdd_HHMMSS>. temporary files removed successful cleanup. directory scratch_dir removed created function.SNPs present GDS file absent summary statistics file still analyzed using Tractor model individual-level data .
